title: Upsert incorrectly sets existing values to `NULL` if the `ON CONFLICT` column is updated
description:
### What happens?

When an `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` statement updates the `ON CONFLICT` column, DuckDB incorrectly sets existing values in the row to `NULL`. See the repro instructions for an example (and how the current behavior differs from Postgres).

### To Reproduce

Create `repro.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE events (id integer PRIMARY KEY, name text);
INSERT INTO events VALUES (1, 'Test 1');
INSERT INTO events (id) VALUES (1) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET id = excluded.id;
SELECT * FROM events;
```

And run:

```sh
duckdb -f repro.sql
```

With DuckDB, this incorrectly sets `name` (and any other columns in the row) to `NULL`:

```text
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  id   в”Ӯ  name   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ varchar в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ   1   в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

With PostgreSQL (`psql -f repro.sql`), this correctly preserves the existing values:

```text
CREATE TABLE
INSERT 0 1
INSERT 0 1
 id |  name  
----+--------
  1 | Test 1
(1 row)
```

### OS:

macOS 15.3.2 arm64

### DuckDB Version:

1.2.1

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Andrew Kane

### Affiliation:

N/A

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a nightly build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (user_id integer PRIMARY KEY, username varchar(255), age integer);
INSERT INTO users (user_id) VALUES (1) ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE username = VALUES(username);
INSERT INTO users (user_id, age) VALUES (2, NULL) ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE age = VALUES(age);
key_bug_features: ON CONFLICT column update sets existing values to NULL
